steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The text has two separate ideas: (1) Endeavour commenced Mission STS-72, and (2) lasting eight days and twenty-two hours, the mission ended when the shuttle landed. The blank must correctly punctuate the boundary between these two sentences.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'STS-72. Lasting': the period ends the first sentence. 'Lasting eight days and twenty-two hours,' is a participial phrase correctly set off by a comma before the independent clause 'the mission ended...' ✓
  • Choice B — 'STS-72, lasting': places 'the mission ended when the shuttle landed' after only a comma — a comma splice between two independent clauses. ✗
  • Choice C — 'STS-72 lasting': no punctuation at all before the participial phrase, making the sentence a run-on. ✗
  • Choice D — 'STS-72, it lasted': 'it lasted eight days and twenty-two hours, the mission ended...' creates a comma splice between two independent clauses. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
